Name: Startup Counsel
Notes: Need Florida-based or remote-friendly firm with genuine startup practice. Avoid generalist business attorneys. Candidates to evaluate: Lowndes (Orlando), Gunster (Miami/statewide), Carlton Fields (Tampa/Miami), or out-of-state options like Cooley/Wilson Sonsini/Fenwick if budget allows.
Linked Initiatives: Form Delaware C-Corp,Register Foreign Entity in Florida,Appoint Florida Registered Agent,Draft Founder Stock Purchase Agreement,Execute Founder IP Assignment Agreement,Collect Contractor IP Assignments,Source Code Ownership Audit,Draft Founder Employment Agreement,Establish NDA Template Library,Draft Customer Terms of Service and Privacy Policy,Obtain Spousal Consent on Founder Stock,Establish Stock Option Plan

Name: Counsel
Notes: Specialized trademark firms are typically more cost-effective than full-service firms. Look for firms doing flat-fee filings. Gerben IP (national), Cogent Law, or trademark practice within startup counsel firm if available.
Cost: $500-1500 clearance search; $750-1500 per class filing including counsel
Linked Initiatives: Conduct Trademark Clearance Search,File Trademark Application (Intent to Use)

Name: Patent Counsel
Notes: Only needed if genuinely novel technical IP exists. Most software patents fail Alice scrutiny. Defer until technical differentiation is validated. When needed: registered patent attorney with software/computer science background.

Name: CPA
Notes: Startup-experienced CPA familiar with R&D credit, QSBS, Delaware franchise tax. Avoid generalist bookkeepers. Consider Pilot, Bench, or boutique startup-focused firms. Florida options include Carr Riggs & Ingram, BDO Tampa/Miami.
Linked Initiatives: Engage CPA,Set Up Florida Sales Tax Registration

Name: Registered Agent Service
Notes: Northwest Registered Agent ($125/yr) or Harbor Compliance ($89/yr). Avoid LegalZoom — overpriced. Provides Florida address for service of process and shields founder home address.

Name: Cloud Infrastructure - AWS
Notes: AWS Activate program offers $1-5k credits for startups. Apply once incorporated. Alternatives: GCP for Startups ($2-100k credits), Azure for Startups. Pick one and stick with it — multi-cloud is premature.

Name: Mercury Bank
Notes: Startup-focused banking. No monthly fees, good API, treasury management for runway. Brex is alternative. Avoid Bank of America Wells Fargo Chase for startup banking — high fees, poor integrations.

Name: Carta
Notes: Free tier for early stage (under $5M raised under 25 stakeholders). Pulley is leaner alternative. Both handle 409A valuations when needed. Avoid spreadsheet cap tables — diligence killer.

Name: Vanta
Notes: SOC2 automation platform. Drata is direct competitor. Only engage when enterprise sales pipeline justifies it. 4-6 months to Type I report. Founder-friendly pricing for early stage.

Name: Gusto
Notes: Payroll and benefits for first US hires. Easy setup, good UI, handles multi-state. Justworks alternative if first hire wants robust benefits.

Name: 1Password Business
Notes: Credential management critical to operational resilience. 1Password Business or Bitwarden Teams. Solves founder-as-single-point-of-failure for access.

Name: Cloudflare
Notes: Free tier covers DNS DDoS basic security. Critical that DNS sits with company not contractor. Enable DNSSEC and access controls.

Name: Vouch Insurance
Notes: Startup-specific insurance broker. Quotes general liability cyber D&O. Embroker is alternative. Avoid generalist commercial insurance brokers — they do not understand startup risk profiles.
